Mr. Glasser stated that they've made progress, that Mr. Der is in receipt of a cashier's check which is now in an escrow account, and that the original transfer document was sent to Ardel (trust company) for a determination as to what still needs to be paid on the condo. In addition, Mr. Glasser stated that Shellaney Masters has an obligation to pay the the property taxes & homeowner's fee and that there is an issue with regard to the condition of the condo, specifically the floor. Mr. Der stated that Ardell will receive the original letter which has been sent, that Ardel will transfer the other fees that need to be paid, that renewal fees are still owing in Delaware, and that he has created an allocation expense sheet which has been provided to Mr. Glasser. In addition, Mr. Der stated that they are moving forward, that it is just a matter of transferring fees, that his client is on his way to Sao Paolo, and that he did receive the $75,000.00 which is sitting in a trust account. Mr. Glasser stated that they want an agreement to inspect the condo. Mr. Der stated that he would need to speak with his client, that this was first time he was hearing about the issue with the floor, and that his client showed him a photo of the condo which showed that the condo was "broom clean". Discussions regarding a possible inspection and the floor were held. Mr. Glasser proposed that an attorney in Portugal do the inspection. Ms. Masters stated that she would pay for an attorney to do the inspection, that the attorney's name is Miguel Cardoso, that Mr. Cardoso has been involved with the family for years, and that he has been to the property. The Court continued the matter to August 27, 2014 at 3:00 pm in Dept. 204 for Mr. Der to contact his client.

Judge Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: None present. (Not Reported)

Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To Ccp 664.6
Off Calendar Jul-17-2014 Continued To Aug-07-14 At 03:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Glasser for and with Sheryl Masters. Ernest Der appeared via telephone for Richard Masters.

Ms. Masters expressed concern about the condition of the Portugal property stating that she saw photos in which the flooring was damaged. The parties are at a standstill with regard to transfer of the property and exchange of funds. Ms. Masters requested an inspection of the property. There is a dispute as to who will pay the transfer fees and or pay for any damages to the property. Mr. Der argues that said issues are not part of the settlement agreement. The parties agreed to the following: (1) Ms. Masters will turn over 2 cashiers checks to Mr. Der to be held in his client trust account in the amounts of $70,000.00 and $5,000.00. (2) Mr. Der will submit the transfer papers to Ardel (holding company). (3) Target date for transfer is July 31, 2014. Counsel are to communicate as to the condition of the property and when the transfer is going forward. The motion to enforce settlement was continued to August 7, 2014 at 3:00 p.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. (Not Reported)

Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To Ccp 664.6
Off Calendar Jun-10-2014 Continued To Jul-17-14 At 03:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To Ccp 664.6
Off Calendar May-14-2014 Continued To Jun-12-14 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Frassetto for Sheryl Masters. Ernest Der for Richard Masters.

The Court questioned where things stand. Mr. Frassetto stated that it looks like things are going to work out, that it looks like Richard Masters is going to comply and that the only thing before the Court is to get a judgment. Mr. Der argued that it is premature for a judgment, that they need to give Richard a chance to perform and that he has provided and explained to Mr. Glasser with the condition and information of the Portugal property and that it will take some time for the Ardel Trust to transfer the title of Romeo Limited. The Court continued this Motion to June 12, 2014 at 2:30 p.m., in Dept. 204 for compliance of the settlement.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: None. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order To Obtain Information Regarding A Trust; For An Accounting; Petition For Instructions Regarding Bequest; Probate Code Sec 16061;
Off Calendar Oct-04-2013 Continued To Dec-12-13 At 01:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With Demand For Inspection Of Real Property
Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Frassetto for Sheryl Masters and Raymond Masters (Petitioners/Moving Parties). Ernest Der for Richard Masters (Respondent).

Mr. Der stated that his client will have someone in Portugal to allow access to the property for inspection and that he lives in Sao Paulo. Mr. Frassetto stated that two other heirs (Leslie and Sidney) live in Portugal and he suggested that they can be there for the inspection. Mr. Der stated that he will do everything that he can to have his client complied. Discussions were held with regard to Richard's availability and the Court's calendar for trial. The Court expressed concern regarding the length of the trial due to the complicated issues. Mr. Frassetto confirmed that the trial will be completed in two one-half days. Mr. Der reported that this case has been to mediation without success, that he suggested to have a settlement conference with Pang Ly. The Court took a short recess to confirm with Ms. Ly regarding her schedule. Settlement Conference with Pang Ly is set on December 11, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. The Court will contact counsel with the details. Trial date is continued to December 12, 2013 at 1:00 p.m. in Dept. 204. Trial brief is due by December 9, 2013. The Court granted the Motion to Compel Compliance with Demand for Inspection of Real Property (The Court will issue an order). Inspection to be completed by October 31, 2013.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Dec-05-2012 Continued To Dec-19-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Glasser for Sheryl Masters. Ernest Der for Richard Masters (Trustee).

Mr. Glasser stated that he presented a settlement proposal to Mr. Der two weeks ago, that Mr. Der was discussing it with his Client, that he has not received any proposal and requested that the matter be set for trial. Mr. Der explained difficulty in communicating with his Client now that the Client resides in Brazil, stated that they are close to getting a counter proposal together and requested a couple of weeks. The Petition for Order to Obtain Information Regarding a Trust, etc. was continued to December 19, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on settlement discussions.
